item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Pathway from bus stop is overgrown causing walkers to step into the road to get through. This is highly dangerous, 18th June
item.description:
From the Badgeworth bus stop to Badgeworth Village the verge has overgrown the pathway such that users are forced to step into the road in some parts. It needs cutting back urgently before something bad happens.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Badgeworth Road, Badgeworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Innsworth Lane is extremely dangerous for pedestrians, especially at school times, and needs traffic lights or at least a pedestrian crossing., 18th June
item.description:
I am writing to request the installation of a pedestrian crossing on Innsworth Lane due to serious road safety concerns. The current situation is extremely dangerous for pedestrians, particularly for children and families who need to cross the road daily. Innsworth Lane carries heavy and fast-moving traffic, and there are no safe crossing facilities available. This issue becomes especially critical during school commuting hours, when large numbers of children are forced to cross the road without any form of protection. The lack of a designated crossing point means pedestrians must judge gaps in traffic, which is highly unsafe. As a result, there is a constant and unacceptable risk of accidents or near misses. This situation could be significantly improved with the installation of traffic lights or, at the very least, a clearly marked pedestrian (zebra) crossing. Urgent action is required to improve safety and prevent a potentially serious incident.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass blocking view, 18th June
item.description:
End of Mottershead Drive, pulling out onto Innsworth Lane. View blocked by tall grass
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public Safety - lack of safe pedestrian crossing, 18th June
item.description:
I am writing to formally request that Gloucestershire County Council Highways assesses and installs a suitable pedestrian crossing (e.g. zebra or signal-controlled crossing) on Innsworth Lane in response to increasing safety concerns. This road is now serving a significantly growing residential population, with a corresponding rise in pedestrian movement—particularly among primary and secondary school-aged children who are required to cross this busy route daily. Nature of the Problem Innsworth Lane functions as a main through-road, carrying consistent and, at peak times, heavy traffic volumes. Vehicles frequently travel at speed, and there are limited safe crossing points along the route. The issue is particularly acute during morning and afternoon school commute periods, where children and families attempt to cross without any formal pedestrian priority measures. Key concerns include: •Lack of designated crossing facilities, forcing pedestrians to judge gaps in fast-moving traffic • High traffic density and speed, reducing safe crossing opportunities • Limited visibility in certain areas, further increasing risk • Absence of traffic-calming infrastructure that would support safer pedestrian movement Impact on Children and Families The most pressing concern is the safety of school-aged children. Many children must cross Innsworth Lane to access local primary and secondary schools, bus stops, or safe walking routes. This creates several risks: • Children may misjudge vehicle speed and distance • Groups of children crossing together can cause sudden stops in traffic or unsafe crossing behaviour • Parents with younger children (including pushchairs) face significant difficulty crossing safely • Vulnerable users, including elderly residents, also face increased danger Without a controlled crossing, the current situation places these road users at ongoing and preventable risk of accidents or near misses.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

Bus stop sign obscured by vegetation, ladders required

item.title: Another pothole - poor repair done in the past, 18th June
item.description:
This pothole is very close to the house - the road was never intended to be used by so much traffic and is deteriorating daily. Lorries are so wide that they are getting closer and closer to the Grade II* listed farmhouse. Cracks are appearing all over the house - possibly related to climate change, likely related to heavy, fast and unnecessary traffic travelling along a lane not intended for such heavy use.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheltenham Road, Coberley, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole, 18th June
item.description:
One of several large potholes that cars and vans are trying to avoid.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coberley, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This pothole is 13cm deep, 1 metre long and a foot or more wide, 18th June
item.description:
This pothole was reported in March and nothing was done about it. It is now 13cm deep and very few cars or vans can avoid it as it creeps across the road. It isn't helped that cars and vans are trying to avoid the overhanging branches of the beech trees and are eroding the road and grass further in an attempt to avoid a flat tyre. There are a number of potholes along this section of the road - I shall send futher reports.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coberley, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 18th June
item.description:
Loose and rocking slab x 4 Please lift and relay 600x600mm x 4 Standard TM with temp fw closure Stats (FW Rocking/Unstable Slab)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous crossing requires zebra crossing, 18th June
item.description:
Nature of the Problem: Innsworth Lane functions as a main through-road, carrying consistent and, at peak times, heavy traffic volumes. Vehicles frequently travel at speed, and there are limited safe crossing points along the route. The issue is particularly acute during morning and afternoon school commute periods, where children and families attempt to cross without any formal pedestrian priority measures. Key concerns include: Lack of designated crossing facilities, forcing pedestrians to judge gaps in fast-moving traffic, High traffic density and speed, reducing safe crossing opportunities, Limited visibility in certain areas, further increasing risk, Absence of traffic-calming infrastructure that would support safer pedestrian movement. Impact on Children and Families: The most pressing concern is the safety of school-aged children. Many children must cross Innsworth Lane to access local primary and secondary schools, bus stops, or safe walking routes. This creates several risks: 1. Children may misjudge vehicle speed and distance 2. Groups of children crossing together can cause sudden stops in traffic or unsafe crossing behaviour 3. Parents with younger children (including pushchairs) face significant difficulty crossing safely 4. Vulnerable users, including elderly residents, also face increased danger Population Growth and Increased Demand: Recent and ongoing housing developments in and around Innsworth have led to a measurable increase in footfall and traffic. This growth has not been matched with adequate pedestrian infrastructure. As the area continues to expand, the pressure on existing road safety conditions will only increase. A failure to act now may result in: Increased likelihood of accidents Reduced willingness for children to walk or cycle to school Greater reliance on car journeys, contributing to congestion.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
